# Synthea sample C-CDA fixture

`synthea_ccda_sample.xml`

- **Source:** [synthetichealth/synthea-sample-data](https://github.com/synthetichealth/synthea-sample-data),
  extracted from `downloads/latest/synthea_sample_data_ccda_latest.zip`
  (file `Darryl392_Gislason620_3da68fbe-0b08-2747-dc0e-437b9970c8cd.xml`, 35 KB —
  the smallest sample in the archive).
- **Generator:** Synthea(TM) Synthetic Patient Population Simulator,
  <https://github.com/synthetichealth/synthea>.
- **License:** Apache License, Version 2.0 —
  <https://github.com/synthetichealth/synthea/blob/master/LICENSE>.
  Copyright The MITRE Corporation.
- **Data type:** fully synthetic; no real patient data. Per the
  [Synthea FAQ](https://github.com/synthetichealth/synthea/wiki/Frequently-Asked-Questions):
  "No individual-level data was used in creating it, so there is no risk of
  any real individual's data being present."

Why this fixture exists: every other C-CDA fixture in this repo was written
by this repo's own authors, so ingest tests could in principle share blind
spots with the parser. This file was generated by an INDEPENDENT tool (MDHT
CDA libraries, US Realm Header + CCD templateIds), making it the
external-evidence lane: if our parser mishandles real-world-shaped CDA, this
is where it shows up first.

The file's Synthea-generated GUIDs (the `3da68fbe-…` family) are individually
allowlisted in `tools/phi_allowlist.txt` with this provenance as the
justification; the PHI scanner's other checks still apply to this file.
